rules.__comment_section_1__: === GLOBAL RULES — apply across ALL tasks ===

no-var-use-explicit-types.note: NEVER use `var` in generated examples. Always declare the explicit type. This is rule #1 for code style — all examples must use explicit type declarations for clarity and discoverability. The only exception is anonymous types in LINQ projections where no explicit type exists.

default-appearance-font-is-readonly-and-.note: DefaultAppearance.Font is a read-only property — it cannot be assigned via object initializer. Use the constructor overload that accepts font name and size: new DefaultAppearance(string fontName, double fontSize, Color textColor). Also, there is no 'FontColor' property; the correct property name is 'TextColor' or pass the color via the constructor.
errors: CS0200: Property or indexer 'DefaultAppearance.Font' cannot be assigned to -- it is read only
CS0117: 'DefaultAppearance' does not contain a definition for 'FontColor'

no-destination-class-use-explicit-destin.note: There is no class called 'Destination' in Aspose.Pdf. To set a link annotation destination, use one of the explicit destination subclasses: XYZExplicitDestination, FitExplicitDestination, GoToAction, etc. ExplicitDestination does not have a static CreateFit method either.
errors: CS0246: The type or namespace name 'Destination' could not be found (are you missing a using directive or an assembly reference?)
CS0117: 'ExplicitDestination' does not contain a definition for 'CreateFit'

no-form-presentation-mode.note: FormPresentationMode enum and PdfViewer.FormPresentationMode property do not exist in Aspose.Pdf. PdfViewer from Aspose.Pdf.Facades does not have a FormPresentationMode property. When printing PDFs with form fields using PdfViewer, simply bind the PDF and call PrintDocument() without trying to set any mode.
errors: CS0234: The type or namespace name 'FormPresentationMode' does not exist in the namespace 'Aspose.Pdf' (are you missing an assembly reference?)

hyperlink-property-is-not-a-string.note: LinkAnnotation.Hyperlink is of type Aspose.Pdf.Hyperlink, not a string. You cannot assign a URL string directly. To create a link to an external URL, use GoToURIAction as the Action, not the Hyperlink property.
errors: CS0029: Cannot implicitly convert type 'string' to 'Aspose.Pdf.Hyperlink'

no-pcl-save-options.note: PclSaveOptions does not exist in Aspose.Pdf. PCL is only supported as an input format (via PclLoadOptions), NOT as an output format. You cannot save/export a PDF to PCL. If PCL output is requested, inform the user that this format is not supported for export.
errors: CS0246: The type or namespace name 'PclSaveOptions' could not be found (are you missing a using directive or an assembly reference?)

no-ps-save-options.note: PsSaveOptions does not exist in Aspose.Pdf. PostScript (PS) is only supported as an input format (via PsLoadOptions), NOT as an output format. You cannot save/export a PDF to PS.
errors: CS0246: The type or namespace name 'PsSaveOptions' could not be found (are you missing a using directive or an assembly reference?)

no-graphic-element-collection.note: GraphicElementCollection does not exist in Aspose.Pdf. To add shapes to a page, use Aspose.Pdf.Drawing.Graph container with Shape subclasses (Rectangle, Circle, Line, etc.), then add the Graph to page.Paragraphs.
errors: CS0246: The type or namespace name 'GraphicElementCollection' could not be found (are you missing a using directive or an assembly reference?)

no-subpath-collection-or-add-vector-grap.note: SubPathCollection does not exist in Aspose.Pdf. Page.AddVectorGraphics() does not exist. SubPath has no parameterless constructor and does not have MoveTo/LineTo/Add/CloseFigure/ClosePath/IsClosed methods. For drawing vector shapes, use Aspose.Pdf.Drawing.Graph with Shape subclasses.
errors: CS0246: The type or namespace name 'SubPathCollection' could not be found (are you missing a using directive or an assembly reference?)
CS0246: The type or namespace name 'SubPath' could not be found (are you missing a using directive or an assembly reference?)
CS1729: 'SubPath' does not contain a constructor that takes 0 arguments
CS1061: 'SubPath' does not contain a definition for 'MoveTo'
CS1061: 'SubPath' does not contain a definition for 'LineTo'
CS1061: 'SubPath' does not contain a definition for 'Add'
CS1061: 'SubPath' does not contain a definition for 'CloseFigure'
CS1061: 'SubPath' does not contain a definition for 'ClosePath'
CS1061: 'SubPath' does not contain a definition for 'IsClosed'
CS1061: 'Page' does not contain a definition for 'AddVectorGraphics'

drawing-rectangle-properties.note: Aspose.Pdf.Drawing.Rectangle does not have FillColor, StrokeColor, LineWidth, BorderColor, or BorderWidth as direct properties. Set visual properties through the GraphInfo property instead. The Drawing.Rectangle constructor takes (left, bottom, width, height), NOT (llx, lly, urx, ury). Also, Drawing.Rectangle cannot be added to GraphicElementCollection or used as GraphicElement — use Graph.Shapes.Add().
errors: CS1061: 'Rectangle' does not contain a definition for 'FillColor'
CS1061: 'Rectangle' does not contain a definition for 'StrokeColor'
CS1061: 'Rectangle' does not contain a definition for 'LineWidth'
CS1061: 'Rectangle' does not contain a definition for 'BorderColor'
CS1061: 'Rectangle' does not contain a definition for 'BorderWidth'
CS0117: 'Rectangle' does not contain a definition for 'FillColor'
CS0117: 'Rectangle' does not contain a definition for 'Border'
CS1503: Argument 1: cannot convert from 'Aspose.Pdf.Drawing.Rectangle' to 'Aspose.Pdf.Vector.GraphicElement'

drawing-ellipse-properties.note: Aspose.Pdf.Drawing.Ellipse does not have FillColor, StrokeColor, LineWidth, BorderColor, or BorderWidth as direct properties. Set visual properties through the GraphInfo property instead. The Ellipse constructor takes (left, bottom, width, height).
errors: CS1061: 'Ellipse' does not contain a definition for 'FillColor'
CS1061: 'Ellipse' does not contain a definition for 'StrokeColor'
CS1061: 'Ellipse' does not contain a definition for 'LineWidth'
CS0117: 'Ellipse' does not contain a definition for 'BorderWidth'
CS0117: 'Ellipse' does not contain a definition for 'BorderColor'

drawing-line-constructor.note: Aspose.Pdf.Drawing.Line constructor takes a float array of coordinates, NOT two Point objects. The array format is { x1, y1, x2, y2 }. Line also does not have Color or Width as direct properties — use GraphInfo.
errors: CS1729: 'Line' does not contain a constructor that takes 2 arguments
CS0117: 'Line' does not contain a definition for 'Color'
CS0117: 'Line' does not contain a definition for 'Width'

no-common-figure-annotation.note: CommonFigureAnnotation is an abstract class and cannot be instantiated directly. Use a concrete subclass like SquareAnnotation, CircleAnnotation, PolygonAnnotation, or PolylineAnnotation. FigureAnnotation also does not exist as a class.
errors: CS0144: Cannot create an instance of the abstract type or interface 'CommonFigureAnnotation'
CS0246: The type or namespace name 'FigureAnnotation' could not be found (are you missing a using directive or an assembly reference?)

using-namespace-not-type.note: Do not write 'using Aspose.Pdf.LoadOptions' — LoadOptions is a class, not a namespace. All load options (OfdLoadOptions, PclLoadOptions, etc.) are in the Aspose.Pdf namespace directly. Use 'using Aspose.Pdf;' instead.
errors: CS0138: A 'using namespace' directive can only be applied to namespaces; 'LoadOptions' is a type not a namespace. Consider a 'using static' directive instead

structure-element-page-is-readonly.note: StructureElement.Page is a read-only property. You cannot assign a Page to a FigureElement or other structure element. The page association is managed internally by the tagged content API.
errors: CS0200: Property or indexer 'StructureElement.Page' cannot be assigned to -- it is read only

artifact-rectangle-is-readonly.note: Artifact.Rectangle is a read-only property. You cannot set it via object initializer. Set the artifact's position using the SetPageNumberPosition or other methods, or use the Artifact constructor that accepts positioning parameters.
errors: CS0200: Property or indexer 'Artifact.Rectangle' cannot be assigned to -- it is read only

artifact-type-no-path-subtype.note: Artifact.ArtifactType does not have a 'Path' value. Artifact.ArtifactSubtype does not have a 'Path' value. Valid ArtifactType values include Background, Layout, Pagination, and others. For drawing shapes, use the Drawing.Graph approach, not artifacts.
errors: CS0117: 'Artifact.ArtifactType' does not contain a definition for 'Path'
CS0117: 'Artifact.ArtifactSubtype' does not contain a definition for 'Path'

no-graphic-state-parameterless-ctor.note: Aspose.Pdf.Vector.GraphicState has no parameterless constructor and does not have StrokeColor or LineWidth as direct static members. For styling vector elements, use GraphInfo on Drawing shapes instead.
no-graphic-state-parameterless-ctor.code:
// WRONG:
// GraphicState gs = new GraphicState { StrokeColor = Color.Green, LineWidth = 3 }; // CS1729, CS0117
errors: CS1729: 'GraphicState' does not contain a constructor that takes 0 arguments
CS0117: 'GraphicState' does not contain a definition for 'StrokeColor'
CS0117: 'GraphicState' does not contain a definition for 'LineWidth'

image-constructor-and-watermark-annotati.note: Aspose.Pdf.Image does not have a constructor that takes a string path. To create an Image, use the parameterless constructor and set the File property. WatermarkAnnotation does not have an 'Image' property for setting appearance. For watermarks with images, use WatermarkArtifact or stamp-based approaches.
errors: CS1729: 'Image' does not contain a constructor that takes 1 arguments
CS0117: 'WatermarkAnnotation' does not contain a definition for 'Image'

ximage-no-imagestream-property.note: XImage does not have an 'ImageStream' property. To reuse a single image resource multiple times on a page, add it once via page.Resources.Images.Add(stream) and then reference it by name using page.AddImage() with the same stream.
errors: CS1061: 'XImage' does not contain a definition for 'ImageStream'

no-image-fragment-class.note: ImageFragment does not exist in Aspose.Pdf. To add an image to a page, use Aspose.Pdf.Image with File or FileStream and add it to page.Paragraphs, or use page.AddImage() with a stream and rectangle.
errors: CS0246: The type or namespace name 'ImageFragment' could not be found (are you missing a using directive or an assembly reference?)

no-set-text-style-on-free-text-annotatio.note: FreeTextAnnotation does not have a SetTextStyle method. To format text in a FreeTextAnnotation, set the DefaultAppearance via the constructor or DefaultAppearance property. Use DefaultAppearance(fontName, fontSize, color) to control font styling.
errors: CS1503: Argument 4: cannot convert from 'Aspose.Pdf.Color' to 'System.Drawing.Color'

document-is-tagged-does-not-exist.note: Document does not have an 'IsTagged' property. To check if a document has tagged content, access doc.TaggedContent directly and handle accordingly. The using directives for tagged content are: Aspose.Pdf.Tagged and Aspose.Pdf.LogicalStructure.
errors: CS1061: 'Document' does not contain a definition for 'IsTagged'

no-graphic-properties-class.note: GraphicProperties class does not exist in Aspose.Pdf. Drawing shapes use the GraphInfo property for visual styling (FillColor, Color, LineWidth).
errors: CS0246: The type or namespace name 'GraphicProperties' could not be found (are you missing a using directive or an assembly reference?)

no-ofd-save-format.note: SaveFormat.OFD does not exist. OFD is only supported as an input format (via OfdLoadOptions). You cannot save a document as OFD. Save as PDF instead.
errors: CS0117: 'SaveFormat' does not contain a definition for 'OFD'

document-disposal-with-using.note: Always wrap Document objects in 'using' statements for deterministic disposal and to avoid file handle leaks. When merging PDFs, both source and target Documents must be in using blocks. Target must stay alive until Save() completes.

page-indexing-one-based.note: Aspose.Pdf uses 1-based page indexing. doc.Pages[1] is the first page. doc.Pages[0] throws IndexOutOfRangeException. Always iterate from 1 to doc.Pages.Count inclusive.

save-to-non-pdf-always-use-save-options.note: Document.Save(string path) WITHOUT a SaveOptions argument ALWAYS writes PDF regardless of the file extension. To save to any non-PDF format you MUST pass an explicit SaveOptions subclass or SaveFormat enum. This applies to HTML, SVG, DOC, DOCX, XLS, XLSX, PPTX, EPUB, XML, and all other formats.

all-save-options-in-aspose-pdf-namespace.note: ALL SaveOptions subclasses (HtmlSaveOptions, SvgSaveOptions, DocSaveOptions, XlsSaveOptions, ExcelSaveOptions, PptxSaveOptions, EpubSaveOptions, XmlSaveOptions, MobiXmlSaveOptions, etc.) are defined in the Aspose.Pdf namespace. There is NO 'Aspose.Pdf.Saving' sub-namespace — it does not exist. Never add 'using Aspose.Pdf.Saving;'.

html-and-image-conversion-gdi-plus-windo.note: HTML-to-PDF, PDF-to-HTML, and some image-based conversions via Aspose.Pdf require GDI+ (Windows only). On macOS and Linux these throw TypeInitializationException or DllNotFoundException. Always wrap such conversions in try-catch when targeting cross-platform deployments.

do-not-use-system-drawing-color.note: Do NOT use System.Drawing.Color in Aspose.Pdf code. System.Drawing is a Windows-only GDI+ library. Use Aspose.Pdf.Color instead — it is cross-platform. If both namespaces are imported, fully qualify as Aspose.Pdf.Color to avoid CS0104 ambiguity.

rectangle-disambiguation.note: When both Aspose.Pdf and Aspose.Pdf.Drawing namespaces are imported, 'Rectangle' becomes ambiguous (CS0104). Always fully qualify as Aspose.Pdf.Rectangle for page/annotation coordinates. Aspose.Pdf.Drawing.Rectangle is for drawing shapes only.

fqtn-for-ambiguous-types.note: Use Fully Qualified Type Names (FQTN) for any Aspose.Pdf type that could clash with BCL or System.Drawing types. Most common ambiguous types: Color (use Aspose.Pdf.Color), Rectangle (use Aspose.Pdf.Rectangle), Font (use Aspose.Pdf.Text.Font). When in doubt, always prefix with the full namespace to avoid CS0104 'ambiguous reference' errors. Never import both Aspose.Pdf and System.Drawing namespaces unless necessary.

encryption-always-use-CryptoAlgorithm.note: Always use CryptoAlgorithm enum (Aspose.Pdf namespace) for Document.Encrypt(). NEVER use CryptographicStandard.Pkcs7 — that is for digital signatures only. Decrypt() takes NO parameters. Valid CryptoAlgorithm values: RC4x40, RC4x128, AESx128, AESx256. Prefer AESx256.

no-aspose-pdf-security-namespace.note: Do NOT add 'using Aspose.Pdf.Security;' — this namespace does not exist in Aspose.Pdf for .NET. CryptoAlgorithm and Permissions enums are in the root Aspose.Pdf namespace.

CS0246 – The type or namespace name 'Cry.fix: Use CryptoAlgorithm enum (Aspose.Pdf) instead. Remove any 'using Aspose.Pdf.Security;'.
CS0246 – The type or namespace name 'Cry.note: CryptographicStandard.Pkcs7 is a digital-signature concept, not a symmetric encryption algorithm.

CS1503 – cannot convert from 'Aspose.Pdf.code: doc.Encrypt(user, owner, perms, CryptoAlgorithm.AESx256); // was CryptographicStandard.Pkcs7
workarounds: Use CryptoAlgorithm.RC4x128 for older reader compatibility

CS1501 – No overload for method 'Decrypt.fix: Call Decrypt() with NO parameters. The password is supplied when opening the Document.
CS1501 – No overload for method 'Decrypt.code:
Document enc = new Document(encryptedPath, userPassword);
enc.Decrypt(); // no argument — was: enc.Decrypt(userPassword)

rules.__comment_section_2b__: === FORMAT / API RULES — discovered during batch testing ===

cgm-is-input-only-no-cgmsaveoptions.note: CGM (Computer Graphics Metafile) is an INPUT-only format in Aspose.Pdf. There is no CgmSaveOptions class — you cannot save a PDF as CGM. To process CGM files use CgmLoadOptions to load them into a Document. When a task says 'using CGM format', interpret it as: load the CGM input and save the result as PDF.

epub-save-options-content-recognition-mo.note: When saving a PDF as EPUB using EpubSaveOptions, the ContentRecognitionMode enum is a NESTED enum inside EpubSaveOptions. Always reference it as EpubSaveOptions.RecognitionMode.Flow (or PdfFlow). Never use the unqualified RecognitionMode enum — it will cause CS0246. Also, EpubSaveOptions does not have a 'Title' property — remove it.

pdf-format-conversion-options-pua-strate.note: When using PdfFormatConversionOptions, the PUA processing strategy property is 'PuaSymbolsProcessingStrategy', NOT 'PuaProcessingStrategy'. The correct enum type is PdfFormatConversionOptions.PuaSymbolsProcessingStrategies. Using 'PuaProcessingStrategy' causes CS1061.

text-extraction-use-textabsorber-not-pag.note: There is no Page.ExtractText(TextExtractionOptions) method. To extract text from a PDF page or document, use TextAbsorber with TextExtractionOptions. Apply the absorber via page.Accept(absorber) or pdfDoc.Pages.Accept(absorber), then read absorber.Text.

form-presentation-mode-in-aspose-pdf-nam.note: FormPresentationMode enum is in the Aspose.Pdf namespace, NOT in Aspose.Pdf.Devices. Do not add 'using Aspose.Pdf.Devices' just for FormPresentationMode. Values: FormPresentationMode.Production (default) and FormPresentationMode.Editor.

aspose-pdf-comparison-namespace-version-.note: Aspose.Pdf.Comparison (SideBySidePdfComparer, SideBySideComparisonOptions) was introduced in a recent Aspose.Pdf version. Do not use it for 'basic operations' examples — it may not be available in all target environments. For basic PDF operations: open, read page count, extract text (via TextAbsorber), and save. Keep examples simple and cross-version compatible.

cgm-invalid-format-exception-does-not-ex.note: There is no InvalidCgmFileFormatException class in Aspose.Pdf. When catching errors from CGM loading, use the generic Exception or Aspose.Pdf.PdfException. Do not catch a non-existent exception type.

rules.__comment_section_2c__: === TAGGED PDF / ACCESSIBILITY API RULES (Aspose.Pdf.LogicalStructure namespace) ===

tagged-content-access-via-doc-taggedcont.note: Access tagged content via doc.TaggedContent — NOT by casting Document to ITaggedContent. Document does NOT implement ITaggedContent. ITaggedContent is in Aspose.Pdf.Tagged namespace.

tagged-pdf-namespaces-logicalstructure-i.note: For tagged PDFs, use 'Aspose.Pdf.LogicalStructure' namespace for all structure element types: StructureElement (root type), ParagraphElement, FigureElement, HeaderElement, DivElement, TableElement, TableTHElement, TableTDElement, etc. 'Aspose.Pdf.Tagged' provides ITaggedContent. Do NOT use 'Aspose.Pdf.Structure' as the primary namespace for structure elements — types from that namespace (like RootElement) cause InvalidCastException when cast from taggedContent.RootElement.

tagged-root-element-structureelement-no-.note: taggedContent.RootElement returns Aspose.Pdf.LogicalStructure.StructureElement directly. Assign it to 'StructureElement' WITHOUT any explicit cast. Never cast to 'RootElement' from Aspose.Pdf.Structure — that throws InvalidCastException at runtime. IMPORTANT: doc.StructTreeRootElement (on the Document class) does NOT exist. However, taggedContent.StructTreeRootElement DOES exist on ITaggedContent and returns Aspose.Pdf.LogicalStructure.StructTreeRootElement — use it only when you specifically need the StructTreeRootElement type. For most structure building, use taggedContent.RootElement (returns StructureElement).

tagged-structure-appendchild-is-correct.note: Add child structure elements using element.AppendChild(child). The actual API signature is AppendChild(Element element, bool checkIfCanBeAppended) — the bool parameter has a default value, so calling with ONE argument is the correct and preferred form. Do NOT use Children.Add() — it does not exist on StructureElement. Avoid calling AppendChild with bool=false as it may reject valid children.

tagged-structure-settext-is-correct-meth.note: Use element.SetText(string) to set the text content of structure elements (ParagraphElement, HeaderElement, TableTHElement, TableTDElement, etc.). This is the confirmed working method. 'ActualText' is a property that also exists on StructureElement but SetText() is the primary method for setting display text.

tagged-figure-alternativetext-property.note: The property for alternate text on FigureElement (and other structure elements) is 'AlternativeText' — NOT '.Alt' or '.AltText'. AlternativeText is defined on Aspose.Pdf.LogicalStructure.StructureElement and applies to all structure element subtypes.

tagged-doc-tagged-and-language-propertie.note: Document has NO 'Tagged' setter (doc.Tagged = true is CS1061) and NO 'IsTagged' setter (doc.IsTagged = true is CS0200 — read-only). There is also NO 'Language' setter (doc.Language = "en-US" is CS1061). Use doc.TaggedContent.SetLanguage() and doc.TaggedContent.SetTitle(). doc.IsTagged is a READ-ONLY boolean property.

tagged-no-presave-needed.note: There is NO requirement to call taggedContent.PreSave() before doc.Save(). Simply call doc.Save(outputPath) to persist all tagged structure changes. PreSave() exists as a method but calling it is NOT required and adds unnecessary code. Just use doc.Save().

tagged-autotaggingsettings-use-static-de.note: AutoTaggingSettings is accessed as a GLOBAL STATIC via AutoTaggingSettings.Default — NOT via doc.TaggedContent.AutoTaggingSettings or doc.AutoTaggingSettings (those instance properties do NOT exist). AutoTaggingSettings is in the Aspose.Pdf namespace (NOT Aspose.Pdf.Tagged) — the standard 'using Aspose.Pdf;' directive already covers it. No additional using is needed.

tagged-ximage-trysetalternativetext-is-v.note: XImage DOES have a TrySetAlternativeText(string altText, Page page) method. This is the PREFERRED way to set alt text on images in a PDF, especially for untagged PDFs — it directly links the alt text to the existing image resource on the page. Always iterate with 'foreach (XImage img in page.Resources.Images)' — NOT as a dictionary.

ximage-collection-foreach-only-no-keys.note: XImageCollection (page.Resources.Images) is NOT a dictionary. Iterate it with 'foreach (XImage img in page.Resources.Images)'. There is NO .Keys property, no KeyValuePair enumeration, and no string-based indexer. The collection directly yields XImage objects.

imagefragment-type-does-not-exist.note: ImageFragment is NOT a valid type in Aspose.Pdf. Do NOT use 'new ImageFragment(...)' — causes CS0246. To work with images: use XImage (from page.Resources.Images), use Image class to add images to pages, or use FigureElement (for tagged structure alt text).

page-structureelements-property-does-not.note: Page does NOT have a StructureElements property. To access the tagged structure tree, use doc.TaggedContent.RootElement and traverse recursively via element.ChildElements. Check doc.IsTagged before accessing TaggedContent on potentially untagged documents.

tagged-table-elements-use-taggedcontent-.note: Table structure elements can be created via two approaches: (1) ITaggedContent factory methods (CreateTableElement, CreateTableTHeadElement, etc.) plus AppendChild() to attach, or (2) TableElement's own CreateTHead()/CreateTBody()/CreateTFoot() methods and TableTRElement's CreateTH()/CreateTD() methods (confirmed via API reflection). In approach 2, the child is automatically attached to the parent. Use SetText() for cell text. Use AlternativeText for table description.

tagged-structure-element-childElements-n.note: To iterate child structure elements, use element.ChildElements (returns IList<Element>). There is NO element.Elements property. Cast each child to StructureElement for typed access.

tagged-structure-findElements-exists-on-.note: Element (the base class of StructureElement) DOES have a FindElements<T>(bool recursiveSearch) method — confirmed by API reflection. Use it to collect all elements of a specific type from the structure tree. Pass 'true' for deep recursive search. This is available on all structure element types (ParagraphElement, FigureElement, HeaderElement, etc.) since they all inherit from Element.

tagged-read-language-via-taggedcontent.note: Read the document language from a tagged PDF via doc.TaggedContent.Language (returns the /Lang string). Do NOT use doc.Language (setter-only pattern) or read from PdfFileInfo.Header (completely wrong). Standard metadata like title/author is in doc.Info.

rules.__comment_section_3__: === TASK-SPECIFIC RULES — added as we test prompts ===

Enable accessibility in PDF by creating.error: Wrong cast: (RootElement)taggedContent.RootElement throws InvalidCastException. Wrong method: Children.Add() does not exist. Wrong namespace: Aspose.Pdf.Structure instead of Aspose.Pdf.LogicalStructure.
Enable accessibility in PDF by creating.fix: Use Aspose.Pdf.LogicalStructure namespace. Assign root as 'StructureElement root = taggedContent.RootElement' with NO explicit cast. Use AppendChild() to attach elements. Use SetText() for text. No PreSave() needed.
Enable accessibility in PDF by creating.note: Aspose.Pdf.LogicalStructure is the correct namespace. StructureElement root = taggedContent.RootElement (no cast). AppendChild() not Children.Add(). SetText() for text. No PreSave().

Enable accessibility in PDF by setting a.error: XImage.TrySetAlternativeText() was incorrectly said to not exist — it DOES exist. XImageCollection is iterated as dictionary (kvp.Value) which is WRONG. FigureElement approach fails on untagged PDFs.
Enable accessibility in PDF by setting a.fix: Use XImage.TrySetAlternativeText(altText, page) to set alt text directly on existing image resources. Iterate page.Resources.Images with 'foreach (XImage img in ...)' — NOT as dictionary.
Enable accessibility in PDF by setting a.note: TrySetAlternativeText(string altText, Page page) IS a valid XImage method. Iterate page.Resources.Images with direct foreach (XImage img in ...) — NOT as a KeyValuePair dictionary.

Access the StructureTreeRoot to iterate.error: Wrong explicit cast (RootElement from Aspose.Pdf.Structure) from taggedContent.RootElement causes InvalidCastException. element.Elements does not exist — correct property is element.ChildElements.
Access the StructureTreeRoot to iterate.fix: Use Aspose.Pdf.LogicalStructure namespace. Assign root as 'StructureElement root = doc.TaggedContent.RootElement' with NO cast. Iterate via element.ChildElements. NOTE: taggedContent.StructTreeRootElement DOES exist (returns Aspose.Pdf.LogicalStructure.StructTreeRootElement), and FindElements<T>(bool) DOES exist on Element — both confirmed by API reflection.
Access the StructureTreeRoot to iterate.note: doc.TaggedContent.RootElement returns StructureElement (no cast needed). taggedContent.StructTreeRootElement DOES exist. FindElements<T>(bool) DOES exist on Element base class. Use element.ChildElements, not element.Elements.

Enhance PDF accessibility by creating a.error: AppendChild(element, false) may reject valid children. Wrong explicit cast (RootElement from Aspose.Pdf.Structure) causes InvalidCastException. Children.Add() doesn't exist. NOTE: taggedContent.StructTreeRootElement DOES exist (returns Aspose.Pdf.LogicalStructure.StructTreeRootElement) — the error was confusing doc.StructTreeRootElement (which doesn't exist) with taggedContent.StructTreeRootElement (which does).
Enhance PDF accessibility by creating a.fix: Use Aspose.Pdf.LogicalStructure. Get root via 'StructureElement root = taggedContent.RootElement' (no cast). Use AppendChild(element) with one arg for all attachments. Create table sub-elements via taggedContent.CreateTableXxxElement() factory OR via table.CreateTHead()/table.CreateTBody() and row.CreateTH()/row.CreateTD() methods. Use SetText() for cell text. Use AlternativeText for table description.
Enhance PDF accessibility by creating a.note: All table elements via taggedContent.CreateTableXxxElement() factory. AppendChild(element) for attachment. StructureElement root = taggedContent.RootElement (no cast). SetText() for cell text. AlternativeText for descriptions.

Extract tagged content from a PDF for ac.error: element.Elements does not exist. Wrong namespace (Aspose.Pdf.Structure). Wrong cast. ITextFragmentProvider not valid on structure elements.
Extract tagged content from a PDF for ac.fix: Use Aspose.Pdf.LogicalStructure namespace. Assign root as 'StructureElement root = taggedContent.RootElement' (no cast). Use element.ChildElements for iteration. Use element.ActualText for text content. Use element.AlternativeText for alt text.
Extract tagged content from a PDF for ac.note: element.ChildElements is the correct property. element.ActualText holds text content. element.AlternativeText holds alt text (NOT .Alt). No GetText() method. Use Aspose.Pdf.LogicalStructure, no cast for root.

Extract language settings from a PDF doc.error: CS1061: doc.Language property (setter-style) doesn't work for reading. PdfFileInfo.Header is not for reading /Lang.
Extract language settings from a PDF doc.fix: Read document language from a tagged PDF via doc.TaggedContent.Language. Read standard metadata (author, title) via doc.Info. Do not use PdfFileInfo.Header to parse /Lang.
Extract language settings from a PDF doc.note: doc.TaggedContent.Language reads the /Lang entry. Standard document info is in doc.Info. PdfFileInfo.Header is NOT for reading /Lang.

Basic operation: Encrypt and Decrypt PDF.error: CS1503/CS0246: CryptographicStandard.Pkcs7 not found. OR CS0234: Aspose.Pdf.Security namespace does not exist.
Basic operation: Encrypt and Decrypt PDF.fix: Remove 'using Aspose.Pdf.Security;'. Use CryptoAlgorithm.AESx256. Use Permissions enum from Aspose.Pdf. Call Decrypt() with no parameters. Wrap Documents in using blocks.
Basic operation: Encrypt and Decrypt PDF.note: CryptoAlgorithm is in Aspose.Pdf. Decrypt() takes no arguments.

Basic operation: Encrypt and Decrypt PDF.error: CS1503: CryptographicStandard.Pkcs7. OR CS0234: Aspose.Pdf.Security / Aspose.Pdf.Saving namespace. OR TypeInitializationException on macOS during HTML save.
Basic operation: Encrypt and Decrypt PDF.fix: Remove 'using Aspose.Pdf.Security;'. Use CryptoAlgorithm.AESx256. Decrypt() takes no params. Pass HtmlSaveOptions explicitly. Wrap HTML save in try-catch.
Basic operation: Encrypt and Decrypt PDF.note: HtmlSaveOptions must be passed explicitly — file extension alone does not trigger HTML output.
workarounds: Wrap HTML save in try-catch for TypeInitializationException on non-Windows

Basic operation: Merge PDF using PDF for.error: Resource leak: source Document not in a using block.
Basic operation: Merge PDF using PDF for.fix: Use nested using blocks for both Documents. Call Save() inside the using scope.
Basic operation: Merge PDF using PDF for.note: Save() is called inside the using scope so neither document is prematurely disposed.

Basic operation: Merge PDF using HTML fo.error: Logic error: Document.Save(path) without SaveOptions always writes PDF even when path ends in .html.
Basic operation: Merge PDF using HTML fo.fix: Pass HtmlSaveOptions explicitly to Document.Save(). Dispose all source Documents with using blocks. Wrap HTML save in try-catch.
Basic operation: Merge PDF using HTML fo.note: HtmlSaveOptions is mandatory — omitting it produces a PDF regardless of the .html extension.

Basic operation: Split PDF using PDF for.error: No compilation error expected. Pitfalls: 0-based page index, Documents not disposed.
Basic operation: Split PDF using PDF for.fix: Pages are 1-based. Use nested using blocks for source and per-page Documents.
Basic operation: Split PDF using PDF for.note: Pages[1] is the first page. Pages[0] throws IndexOutOfRangeException.

Basic operation: Split PDF using HTML fo.error: Resource leak: Document not in using block. OR TypeInitializationException on macOS/Linux.
Basic operation: Split PDF using HTML fo.fix: Wrap Document in using. Pass HtmlSaveOptions with SplitIntoPages=true explicitly. Wrap HTML save in try-catch.
Basic operation: Split PDF using HTML fo.note: SplitIntoPages=true writes one HTML file per page. HtmlSaveOptions must be passed explicitly.
workarounds: Wrap HTML save in try-catch for TypeInitializationException

Basic operation: Convert PDF document fr.error: No error expected. Intent may be: (a) simple copy, (b) PDF/A conversion, (c) optimize, (d) version change.
Basic operation: Convert PDF document fr.fix: For a basic copy use Document.Save(). For PDF/A use Document.Convert(). Always wrap Document in using.
Basic operation: Convert PDF document fr.note: Plain Save() copies the PDF. For PDF/A use doc.Convert(logFile, PdfFormat.PDF_A_1B, ConvertErrorAction.Delete). For optimization use doc.OptimizeResources(new OptimizationOptions()).

Basic operation: Basic operations with P.error: CS0246: SideBySidePdfComparer, SideBySideComparisonOptions not found. OR CS1061: Page does not contain a definition for 'ExtractText'.
Basic operation: Basic operations with P.fix: Do not use Aspose.Pdf.Comparison for basic operations examples. Use TextAbsorber for text extraction.
Basic operation: Basic operations with P.note: Page.ExtractText() does not exist — always use TextAbsorber. Aspose.Pdf.Comparison is an advanced versioned feature — avoid in basic examples.

Basic operation: Basic operations with P.error: TypeInitializationException on macOS/Linux during HTML save.
Basic operation: Basic operations with P.fix: Load PDF, perform basic operations, save as HTML using explicit HtmlSaveOptions. Wrap HTML save in try-catch for GDI+ unavailability.
Basic operation: Basic operations with P.note: Use TextAbsorber for text extraction. HtmlSaveOptions must be passed explicitly. HTML save is Windows-only.

Basic operation: Split PDF using CGM for.error: CS0246: CgmSaveOptions does not exist. CGM is an input-only format in Aspose.Pdf.
Basic operation: Split PDF using CGM for.fix: CGM cannot be used as an output format. Split the PDF into individual PDF pages instead.
Basic operation: Split PDF using CGM for.note: CgmSaveOptions does not exist — CGM is input-only in Aspose.Pdf.
workarounds: Output as PDF (default), SVG, HTML, or XML instead of CGM

Basic operation: Open PDF document progr.fix: Remove the catch for InvalidCgmFileFormatException. Use PdfException or generic Exception instead.
Basic operation: Open PDF document progr.note: InvalidCgmFileFormatException does not exist in Aspose.Pdf. Use PdfException or Exception.

Basic operation: Open PDF document progr.error: CS0246: RecognitionMode not found (must be EpubSaveOptions.RecognitionMode). CS1061: EpubSaveOptions does not contain 'Title'.
Basic operation: Open PDF document progr.fix: ContentRecognitionMode must use the nested enum EpubSaveOptions.RecognitionMode.Flow. Remove the Title property.
Basic operation: Open PDF document progr.note: EpubSaveOptions.RecognitionMode is a nested enum. Values: Flow, PdfFlow, Fixed.

Basic operation: Save PDF/A or PDF/X for.error: CS1061: PdfFormatConversionOptions does not contain 'PuaProcessingStrategy' (wrong property name).
Basic operation: Save PDF/A or PDF/X for.fix: Use the simpler doc.Convert(logFile, PdfFormat, ConvertErrorAction) overload.
Basic operation: Save PDF/A or PDF/X for.note: Prefer doc.Convert(logFile, PdfFormat.PDF_A_1B, ConvertErrorAction.Delete). PdfFormat values: PDF_A_1A, PDF_A_1B, PDF_A_2A, PDF_A_2B, PDF_A_3A, PDF_A_3B, PDF_X_1A, PDF_X_3.

Basic operation: Live Example using PDF.error: CS0246: FormPresentationMode not found — wrong namespace.
Basic operation: Live Example using PDF.fix: FormPresentationMode is in Aspose.Pdf namespace, NOT Aspose.Pdf.Devices.
Basic operation: Live Example using PDF.note: FormPresentationMode is in Aspose.Pdf, not Aspose.Pdf.Devices. Values: Production (default), Editor.

Basic operation: Open existing PDF docum.error: CS0246: InvalidCgmFileFormatException does not exist in Aspose.Pdf.
Basic operation: Open existing PDF docum.fix: Remove catch block for InvalidCgmFileFormatException. Open the CGM file via a FileStream + CgmLoadOptions.
Basic operation: Open existing PDF docum.note: InvalidCgmFileFormatException does not exist in Aspose.Pdf. Use PdfException or Exception.

Basic operation: Convert a document to a.error: Logic error: generated code only exports to XML, skipping the PDF/X-3 conversion step entirely.
Basic operation: Convert a document to a.fix: (1) load PDF, (2) convert to PDF/X-3, (3) save as XML. Both steps are required.
Basic operation: Convert a document to a.note: Both steps are required: Convert() for PDF/X-3, then Save(..., XmlSaveOptions) for XML.

Basic operation: Convert a document to a.error: Potential runtime error: SVG file loaded without SvgLoadOptions.
Basic operation: Convert a document to a.fix: When loading SVG use new Document(svgPath, new SvgLoadOptions()).
Basic operation: Convert a document to a.note: Always use SvgLoadOptions when loading .svg files into Document.

Basic operation: Convert PDF document fr.error: CS0234: 'Saving' namespace not found. OR CS0138: 'using Aspose.Pdf.HtmlLoadOptions' treats a type as a namespace.
Basic operation: Convert PDF document fr.fix: Remove 'using Aspose.Pdf.Saving;' and 'using Aspose.Pdf.HtmlLoadOptions;'. Both SvgSaveOptions and HtmlLoadOptions are in Aspose.Pdf.
Basic operation: Convert PDF document fr.note: SvgSaveOptions is in Aspose.Pdf — not Aspose.Pdf.Saving. HtmlLoadOptions is a class — never use it as a namespace.
workarounds: If HTML uses relative paths: loadOptions.BasePath = Path.GetDirectoryName(Path.GetFullPath(htmlPath));

add-text-annotation-use-textannotation-n.error: CS1503: Argument 1: cannot convert from 'Aspose.Pdf.Rectangle' to 'System.Drawing.Rectangle' | CS0104: 'Rectangle' is an ambiguous reference between 'Aspose.Pdf.Rectangle' and 'System.Drawing.Rectangle'
add-text-annotation-use-textannotation-n.fix: Do NOT use PdfContentEditor.CreateText() to add a text annotation. PdfContentEditor is a Facades API that requires System.Drawing.Rectangle (Windows-only GDI+). Use Aspose.Pdf.Annotations.TextAnnotation directly — it accepts Aspose.Pdf.Rectangle and works cross-platform. Add the annotation to page.Annotations collection.
add-text-annotation-use-textannotation-n.note: PdfContentEditor.CreateText() expects System.Drawing.Rectangle which clashes with Aspose.Pdf.Rectangle causing CS1503/CS0104. The preferred approach is to use TextAnnotation from Aspose.Pdf.Annotations — it is cross-platform and uses Aspose.Pdf.Rectangle natively. Always import Aspose.Pdf.Annotations and use TextIcon for the Icon property.

add-stamp-to-all-pages-call-per-page-not.error: CS1061: 'PageCollection' does not contain a definition for 'AddStamp' and no accessible extension method 'AddStamp' accepting a first argument of type 'PageCollection' could be found
add-stamp-to-all-pages-call-per-page-not.fix: AddStamp() is a method on each individual Page object, NOT on PageCollection. Remove the call to pdfDocument.Pages.AddStamp() and instead iterate over all pages with foreach, calling page.AddStamp(stamp) on each Page.
add-stamp-to-all-pages-call-per-page-not.note: PageCollection (pdfDocument.Pages) does not expose AddStamp(). Only the Page class has this method. The same applies to TextStamp and other stamp types — always iterate pages and stamp each one individually.

document-istagged-and-itaggedcontent-lan.error: CS1061: 'Document' does not contain a definition for 'IsTagged' | CS1061: 'ITaggedContent' does not contain a definition for 'Language' | CS1061: 'ITaggedContent' does not contain a definition for 'Title'
document-istagged-and-itaggedcontent-lan.fix: Document has no IsTagged property. ITaggedContent has no Language or Title getter properties — they are write-only via SetLanguage()/SetTitle(). To check for tagged content use doc.TaggedContent != null. To read language use doc.Info["Lang"]. To read title use doc.Info.Title.
document-istagged-and-itaggedcontent-lan.note: ITaggedContent exposes only write setters: SetLanguage() and SetTitle(). There are no corresponding getter properties. Document.IsTagged does not exist — use doc.TaggedContent != null to detect whether the document has a tagged structure.

structureelement-lang-use-language-prope.error: CS1061: 'ParagraphElement' does not contain a definition for 'Lang' | CS1061: 'StructureElement' does not contain a definition for 'Lang'
structureelement-lang-use-language-prope.fix: The language property on all structure elements (ParagraphElement, FigureElement, SpanElement, etc.) is named Language, not Lang.
structureelement-lang-use-language-prope.note: All Aspose.Pdf.LogicalStructure element types expose a 'Language' string property, not 'Lang'. This matches the PDF specification attribute name 'Lang' in PDF but the C# property is called Language.

textfragment-position-use-aspose-pdf-tex.error: CS0029: Cannot implicitly convert type 'Aspose.Pdf.Point' to 'Aspose.Pdf.Text.Position'
textfragment-position-use-aspose-pdf-tex.fix: TextFragment.Position expects Aspose.Pdf.Text.Position, not Aspose.Pdf.Point. Use new Position(x, y) from the Aspose.Pdf.Text namespace.
textfragment-position-use-aspose-pdf-tex.note: Aspose.Pdf.Point and Aspose.Pdf.Text.Position are different types. The TextFragment.Position property requires Aspose.Pdf.Text.Position. Always add 'using Aspose.Pdf.Text;' when working with TextFragment, TextState, TextAbsorber, or Font.

aspose-pdf-text-namespace-required-for-t.error: CS0246: The type or namespace name 'TextAbsorber' could not be found | CS0246: The type or namespace name 'TextFragment' could not be found | CS0246: The type or namespace name 'Position' could not be found
aspose-pdf-text-namespace-required-for-t.fix: TextAbsorber, TextFragment, TextState, Position, Font, and FontRepository all live in the Aspose.Pdf.Text namespace. Add 'using Aspose.Pdf.Text;' to the file.
aspose-pdf-text-namespace-required-for-t.note: A common mistake is importing only 'using Aspose.Pdf;' and then getting CS0246 for text-related types. TextAbsorber, TextFragment, TextState, Position, Font, and FontRepository are all in Aspose.Pdf.Text, not Aspose.Pdf.

encrypt-use-cryptoalgorithm-not-standard.error: CS1739: The best overload for 'Encrypt' does not have a parameter named 'standard'
encrypt-use-cryptoalgorithm-not-standard.fix: Document.Encrypt() takes positional arguments. The 4th parameter is CryptoAlgorithm (e.g. CryptoAlgorithm.AESx256), not a named 'standard' parameter. Do not use CryptographicStandard — it does not exist. Do not add 'using Aspose.Pdf.Security;' — that namespace does not exist.
encrypt-use-cryptoalgorithm-not-standard.note: The Document.Encrypt overload is: Encrypt(string userPassword, string ownerPassword, Permissions permissions, CryptoAlgorithm cryptoAlgorithm). There is no 'standard' named parameter. CryptographicStandard and Aspose.Pdf.Security do not exist.

structureattributecollection-no-add-use-.error: CS1061: 'StructureAttributeCollection' does not contain a definition for 'Add'
structureattributecollection-no-add-use-.fix: StructureAttributeCollection has no Add() method. For marking a TH cell as a table header, just using TableTHElement is sufficient — the element type (TH) already declares it as a header per the PDF/UA standard. If owner-specific attributes are needed, use CreateAttributes(AttributeOwnerStandard.Table) to obtain a StructureAttributes object.
structureattributecollection-no-add-use-.note: StructureAttributeCollection exposes only GetAttributes(AttributeOwnerStandard) and CreateAttributes(AttributeOwnerStandard). There is no Add() method. TableTHElement inherently marks the cell as a table header; no additional attribute is required for PDF/UA compliance.

textelement-type-not-found-use-structure.error: CS0246: The type or namespace name 'TextElement' could not be found
textelement-type-not-found-use-structure.fix: TextElement does not exist in Aspose.Pdf.LogicalStructure. To iterate over all text-bearing structure elements, use FindElements<StructureElement>(true) with the base StructureElement type. For specific text roles use ParagraphElement, SpanElement, or HeaderElement.
textelement-type-not-found-use-structure.note: There is no 'TextElement' class in Aspose.Pdf.LogicalStructure. The available text structure element types are ParagraphElement, SpanElement, HeaderElement (H1-H6), LinkElement, NoteElement, and their base class StructureElement.

pdffileeditor-not-idisposable-and-has-no.error: CS1674: 'PdfFileEditor': type used in a using statement must implement 'System.IDisposable' | CS1061: 'PdfFileEditor' does not contain a definition for 'BindPdf' | CS1061: 'PdfFileEditor' does not contain a definition for 'Save'
pdffileeditor-not-idisposable-and-has-no.fix: PdfFileEditor does NOT implement IDisposable — never wrap it in a using statement. It also has no BindPdf() or Save() methods. Those belong to Document (core API) or to PdfFileMend/PdfContentEditor (Facades). PdfFileEditor is only for file operations: Concatenate, Split, Extract, Insert. For load/save use Document.
pdffileeditor-not-idisposable-and-has-no.note: PdfFileEditor is a Facades helper for bulk file operations (Concatenate, Split, Extract). It is not an IDisposable and has no document-level BindPdf/Save API. If you need to load/modify/save a PDF use Document instead.

autotaggingsettings-headinglevels-not-in.error: CS0029: Cannot implicitly convert type 'int[]' to 'Aspose.Pdf.HeadingLevels'
autotaggingsettings-headinglevels-not-in.fix: AutoTaggingSettings.Default.HeadingLevels is of type HeadingLevels, not int[]. HeadingLevels constructor takes an optional double threshold (font-size ratio) for auto-detection, not an array of level numbers. Use new HeadingLevels() for defaults.
autotaggingsettings-headinglevels-not-in.note: HeadingLevels is a class (not an enum or int array) whose constructor accepts an optional double threshold used to detect heading levels based on font-size ratios. It does not represent an array of level numbers.

default-appearance-constructor-requires-.note: DefaultAppearance(string fontName, double fontSize, Color textColor) constructor requires System.Drawing.Color as the third parameter, NOT Aspose.Pdf.Color. This is a common mistake because most other Aspose.Pdf APIs use Aspose.Pdf.Color.
errors: CS1503: Argument 3: cannot convert from 'Aspose.Pdf.Color' to 'System.Drawing.Color'

callout-requires-exactly-three-points.note: FreeTextAnnotation.Callout requires exactly 3 points in the Point array: start (inside the annotation), knee (bend), and end (pointing to target). Passing 2 or any other number throws System.ArgumentException at runtime.
errors: System.ArgumentException: Points array must contain 3 items.

elementlist-no-indexof-use-cast-tolist.error: CS1929: 'ElementList' does not contain a definition for 'IndexOf'
elementlist-no-indexof-use-cast-tolist.fix: ElementList has no IndexOf() method. Convert it to a List<Element> first using LINQ Cast<Element>().ToList(), then call IndexOf() on the resulting list.
elementlist-no-indexof-use-cast-tolist.note: ElementList is a read-only enumerable collection that supports Count, indexed access via ElementOf, and GetEnumerator. It has no IndexOf(). Use Cast<Element>().ToList() to get a regular List<Element> that supports IndexOf, Contains, and other LINQ operations.

annotation-collection-one-based-indexing.note: Aspose.Pdf annotation collections use 1-based indexing, same as Pages. page.Annotations[1] is the first annotation. page.Annotations[0] throws an exception. page.Annotations.Delete(index) also expects a 1-based index. Always iterate from 1 to page.Annotations.Count inclusive.

annotation-title-requires-markup-annotat.note: The 'Title' property belongs to MarkupAnnotation, NOT the base Annotation class. When iterating page.Annotations or using FindByName(), the returned type is Annotation which does not have Title. You must cast to MarkupAnnotation before accessing Title. The 'Contents' property IS on the base Annotation class and can be accessed directly.

border-requires-parent-annotation.note: The Border class has no parameterless constructor — it requires an Annotation parent argument: new Border(annotation). Border also has NO Color property; border color is controlled by the annotation's own Color property. Because of the parent parameter requirement, Border cannot be used inside the object initializer of the annotation it belongs to (the variable is not yet assigned). Set Border separately after construction.

qualify-rectangle-and-color.note: Always use fully qualified Aspose.Pdf.Rectangle and Aspose.Pdf.Color to avoid ambiguity with System.Drawing types. Even if System.Drawing is not explicitly referenced, implicit usings or transitive dependencies can pull it in and cause CS0104 ambiguity errors.

stamp-ambiguity-qualify-facades-stamp.note: When both 'using Aspose.Pdf;' and 'using Aspose.Pdf.Facades;' are imported, unqualified 'Stamp' causes CS0104 ambiguity between Aspose.Pdf.Stamp and Aspose.Pdf.Facades.Stamp. For facade-based watermarking, ALWAYS use the fully qualified name Aspose.Pdf.Facades.Stamp.
errors: CS0104: 'Stamp' is an ambiguous reference between 'Aspose.Pdf.Facades.Stamp' and 'Aspose.Pdf.Stamp'

stamp-no-settext-setfont-setfontsize-set.note: Aspose.Pdf.Facades.Stamp does NOT have SetText(), SetFont(), SetFontSize(), SetTextColor(), or SetColor() methods. To add text to a Stamp, create a FormattedText object with the desired text, font, size, and color via its constructor, then bind it using stamp.BindLogo(formattedText). FormattedText also does NOT have settable properties like .Text, .Font, .FontSize, or .TextState — all styling must be passed via the constructor.
errors: CS1061: 'Stamp' does not contain a definition for 'SetText'
CS1061: 'Stamp' does not contain a definition for 'SetFont'
CS1061: 'Stamp' does not contain a definition for 'SetFontSize'
CS1061: 'Stamp' does not contain a definition for 'SetTextColor'
CS1061: 'Stamp' does not contain a definition for 'SetColor'
CS1061: 'FormattedText' does not contain a definition for 'Text'
CS1061: 'FormattedText' does not contain a definition for 'Font'
CS1061: 'FormattedText' does not contain a definition for 'FontSize'
CS1061: 'FormattedText' does not contain a definition for 'TextState'

pdffilestamp-inputfile-outputfile-obsole.note: PdfFileStamp.InputFile and PdfFileStamp.OutputFile are obsolete. Use BindPdf(inputFile) to load the source PDF and Save(outputFile) to write the result. Alternatively, pass a Document object to the PdfFileStamp constructor: new PdfFileStamp(document).
errors: CS0618: 'PdfFileStamp.InputFile' is obsolete: 'Use BindPdf(inputFile) method for facade initialization.'
CS0618: 'PdfFileStamp.OutputFile' is obsolete: 'Use Save(outputFile) method for getting facade results.'

formattedtext-constructor-for-styling.note: FormattedText (Aspose.Pdf.Facades) does NOT have settable properties for Text, Font, FontSize, or TextState. All text content and styling must be set via the constructor. Use AddNewLineText() to append additional lines after construction. The color parameter is System.Drawing.Color, NOT Aspose.Pdf.Color.
errors: CS1061: 'FormattedText' does not contain a definition for 'Text'
CS1061: 'FormattedText' does not contain a definition for 'Font'
CS1061: 'FormattedText' does not contain a definition for 'FontSize'
CS1061: 'FormattedText' does not contain a definition for 'TextState'

stamp-settext-formattedtext-use-bindlogo.note: Stamp.SetText(FormattedText) does not exist. To bind formatted text (with font, color, and size) to a Stamp, use stamp.BindLogo(formattedText). The FormattedText must be fully configured via its constructor before calling BindLogo.
errors: CS1061: 'Stamp' does not contain a definition for 'SetText'

formattedtext-no-addtext-use-addnewlinet.note: FormattedText does NOT have an AddText() method. To append additional lines after construction, use AddNewLineText(). Do NOT prefix the text with '\n' when using AddNewLineText — it automatically starts a new line.
errors: CS1061: 'FormattedText' does not contain a definition for 'AddText'

formattedtext-no-textcolor-or-fontcolor.note: FormattedText does NOT have a TextColor or FontColor property. Text color must be set via the FormattedText constructor using System.Drawing.Color as the second parameter. There are no settable color properties on FormattedText after construction.
errors: CS1061: 'FormattedText' does not contain a definition for 'TextColor'
CS0117: 'FormattedText' does not contain a definition for 'FontColor'

no-markdownloadoptions-use-mdloadoptions.note: There is no class called 'MarkdownLoadOptions' in Aspose.Pdf. To load Markdown files, use MdLoadOptions instead.
errors: CS0246: The type or namespace name 'MarkdownLoadOptions' could not be found (are you missing a using directive or an assembly reference?)

pdfpagestamp-not-compatible-with-pdffile.note: PdfFileStamp.AddStamp() only accepts Aspose.Pdf.Facades.Stamp, NOT PdfPageStamp. PdfPageStamp is a core API class meant for use with Page.AddStamp(). To use a PDF page as a stamp with the Facades API (PdfFileStamp), create an Aspose.Pdf.Facades.Stamp and use stamp.BindPdf(pdfPath, pageNumber) or stamp.BindPdf(stream, pageNumber) to bind a specific page as the stamp content.
errors: CS1503: Argument 1: cannot convert from 'Aspose.Pdf.PdfPageStamp' to 'Aspose.Pdf.Facades.Stamp'

pdfpagestamp-background-not-isbackground.note: PdfPageStamp uses the property name 'Background' (not 'IsBackground') to control whether the stamp appears behind page content. IsBackground does not exist on PdfPageStamp.
errors: CS0117: 'PdfPageStamp' does not contain a definition for 'IsBackground'

pdffilestamp-constructor-string-string-o.note: The PdfFileStamp(string inputFile, string outputFile) constructor is obsolete. Use the parameterless constructor followed by BindPdf() and Save() instead.
errors: CS0618: 'PdfFileStamp.PdfFileStamp(string, string)' is obsolete: 'Use constructor without destination.'

stamp-no-setopacity-use-opacity-property.note: Aspose.Pdf.Facades.Stamp does NOT have a SetOpacity() method. Use the Opacity property directly instead.
errors: CS1061: 'Stamp' does not contain a definition for 'SetOpacity'

epub-requires-epubloadoptions.note: Loading an EPUB file with new Document(epubPath) without specifying EpubLoadOptions will fail or produce incorrect results. Always pass new EpubLoadOptions() as the second argument when loading EPUB files.

fontrepository-requires-using-aspose-pdf.note: FontRepository is in the Aspose.Pdf.Text namespace. If you use FontRepository.FindFont() without importing Aspose.Pdf.Text, you will get CS0103. Add 'using Aspose.Pdf.Text;' or fully qualify as Aspose.Pdf.Text.FontRepository.
errors: CS0103: The name 'FontRepository' does not exist in the current context

html-requires-htmlloadoptions.note: Loading an HTML file with new Document(htmlPath) without HtmlLoadOptions will fail or produce incorrect results. Always pass new HtmlLoadOptions() as the second argument. For HTML strings in memory, write the HTML to a MemoryStream first, then pass the stream with HtmlLoadOptions to the Document constructor.

svg-requires-svgloadoptions.note: Loading an SVG file with new Document(svgPath) without SvgLoadOptions will fail or produce incorrect results. Always pass new SvgLoadOptions() as the second argument. SvgLoadOptions has an AdjustPageSize property that can be set to true to auto-size the PDF page to match the SVG dimensions.

xps-requires-xpsloadoptions.note: Loading an XPS file with new Document(xpsPath) without XpsLoadOptions will fail. Always pass new XpsLoadOptions() as the second argument.

xslfo-requires-xslfoloadoptions.note: Loading an XSL-FO file requires XslFoLoadOptions (not XslFoLoadOption, not XsltLoadOptions, not FoLoadOptions). The class is in the Aspose.Pdf namespace. Always pass new XslFoLoadOptions() as the second argument to the Document constructor.
errors: CS0246: The type or namespace name 'XsltLoadOptions' could not be found
CS0246: The type or namespace name 'FoLoadOptions' could not be found
CS0246: The type or namespace name 'XslFoLoadOption' could not be found

cgm-requires-cgmloadoptions-with-pagesiz.note: Loading a CGM file requires CgmLoadOptions. Optionally pass a System.Drawing.SizeF for the page size. The class is in the Aspose.Pdf namespace.

mht-requires-mhtloadoptions.note: Loading an MHT/MHTML file requires MhtLoadOptions. The class is in the Aspose.Pdf namespace. Always pass new MhtLoadOptions() as the second argument.

pcl-requires-pclloadoptions.note: Loading a PCL file requires PclLoadOptions. The class is in the Aspose.Pdf namespace. PCL files can also be loaded from a stream with PclLoadOptions.

xml-load-use-bindxml-not-document-constr.note: To load XML content into a PDF, use Document.BindXml(xmlPath) or Document.BindXml(xmlStream) instead of passing XML to the Document constructor. The Document constructor does not accept raw XML files without XmlLoadOptions. For XSL-FO XML, use XslFoLoadOptions instead.

ofd-requires-ofdloadoptions.note: Loading an OFD file requires OfdLoadOptions. The class is in the Aspose.Pdf namespace. Always pass new OfdLoadOptions() as the second argument.

tex-requires-texloadoptions.note: Loading a TeX/LaTeX file requires TeXLoadOptions (not TexLoadOptions, not LatexLoadOptions). The class is in the Aspose.Pdf namespace.
errors: CS0246: The type or namespace name 'TexLoadOptions' could not be found
CS0246: The type or namespace name 'LatexLoadOptions' could not be found

ps-requires-psloadoptions.note: Loading a PostScript (.ps) file requires PsLoadOptions. The class is in the Aspose.Pdf namespace.

loadoptions-is-abstract-use-concrete-cla.note: LoadOptions is an abstract base class and cannot be instantiated directly. Always use the concrete derived class that matches the source format: HtmlLoadOptions, SvgLoadOptions, XpsLoadOptions, EpubLoadOptions, MdLoadOptions, MhtLoadOptions, PclLoadOptions, CgmLoadOptions, XslFoLoadOptions, TeXLoadOptions, PsLoadOptions, OfdLoadOptions, etc.
loadoptions-is-abstract-use-concrete-cla.code:
// CORRECT — use the format-specific class:
new HtmlLoadOptions()    // for .html
new SvgLoadOptions()     // for .svg
new XpsLoadOptions()     // for .xps
new EpubLoadOptions()    // for .epub
new MdLoadOptions()      // for .md (Markdown)
new MhtLoadOptions()     // for .mht/.mhtml
new PclLoadOptions()     // for .pcl
new CgmLoadOptions()     // for .cgm
new XslFoLoadOptions()   // for .fo (XSL-FO)
new TeXLoadOptions()     // for .tex
new PsLoadOptions()      // for .ps (PostScript)
new OfdLoadOptions()     // for .ofd

// WRONG — abstract class:
// new LoadOptions()  // CS0144: cannot create instance of abstract class
errors: CS0144: Cannot create an instance of the abstract type or interface 'LoadOptions'

aspose-pdf-tagged-itaggedcontent.code:
## ITaggedContent interface

Represents interface for work with TaggedPdf content of document.

```csharp
public interface ITaggedContent
```

## Properties

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |

```

This method ensures that all tagged content elements are properly updated and saved within the PDF document. It performs necessary operations such as updating MCID for MCR elements, setting BDC operators, and ensuring compliance with PDF/UA standards.

```

## Constructors

| HeadingLevels { get; set; } | Gets or sets the heading levels used for determining the structure of headings in a PDF document. |
| HeadingRecognitionStrategy { get; set; } | Gets or sets the strategy used for recognizing headings in the document during auto-tagging. |

## Remarks

The `AutoTaggingSettings` class allows configuring options for automatic tagging of PDF content. It includes properties to enable or disable auto-tagging, specify a strategy for heading recognition, and define heading levels based on font sizes.

aspose-pdf-pdfformatconversionoptions-au.code:
## PdfFormatConversionOptions.AutoTaggingSettings property

Gets or sets the settings for automatic tagging during PDF format conversion.

```csharp
public AutoTaggingSettings AutoTaggingSettings { get; set; }
```

Automatic tagging settings are used to configure the behavior of the auto-tagging process, which is typically employed to enhance the accessibility and structure of a PDF document during conversion to a specific PDF format.

aspose-pdf-autotaggingsettings-default.code:
## AutoTaggingSettings.Default property

Gets the default settings for auto-tagging functionality in PDF documents.

```csharp
public static AutoTaggingSettings Default { get; }
```

## Remarks

The default settings enable auto-tagging and use the automatic strategy for heading recognition. These settings can be used as a baseline configuration for PDF format conversion or other operations requiring automatic tagging of PDF content.

## HeadingRecognitionStrategy enumeration

```csharp
public enum HeadingRecognitionStrategy
```

### Values

| Name | Value | Description |

| Outlines | `0` | Represents the header recognition strategy by means of outlines. |
| Heuristic | `1` | Represents the header recognition strategy by means of heuristics rules and font size statistic. |

## HtmlSaveOptions.HtmlMarkupGenerationModes enumeration

Sometimes specific reqirments to created HTML are present. This enum defines HTML preparing modes that can be used during conversion of PDF to HTML to match such specific requirments.

```csharp
public enum HtmlMarkupGenerationModes
```

### Values

| Name | Value | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| WriteAllHtml | `0` | Default mode any specific requirments are absent. Will be generated output that will contain all parts of HTML without any special additional processing. |
| WriteOnlyBodyContent | `1` | will be stripped away all HTML content that is outside HTML's body, i.e. will be left only content that is inside \c \ \c \ tags |

## ParagraphAbsorber class

Represents an absorber object of page structure objects such as sections and paragraphs. Performs search for sections and paragraphs of text and provides access for rectangles and polydons that describes it in text coordinate space. Also performs text segments search and provides access to search results via !:TextFragments collections grouped by structure elements.

## StructureTypeStandard.TBody field

(Table body row group; PDF 1.5) A group of rows that constitute the main body portion of a table. If the table is split across multiple pages, the body area may be broken apart on a row boundary. A table may have multiple TBody elements to allow for the drawing of a border or background for a set of rows.

aspose-pdf-logicalstructure-structuretyp.code:
## StructureTypeStandard.Table field

(Table) A two-dimensional layout of rectangular data cells, possibly having a complex substructure. It contains either one or more table rows (structure type TR) as children; or an optional table head (structure type THead) followed by one or more table body elements (structure type TBody) and an optional table footer (structure type TFoot). In addition, a table may have a caption (structure type Caption) as its first or last child.

```csharp
public static readonly StructureTypeStandard Table;
```

## StructureTypeStandard.TFoot field

(Table footer row group; PDF 1.5) A group of rows that constitute the footer of a table. If the table is split across multiple pages, these rows may be redrawn at the bottom of each table fragment (although there is only one TFoot element.)

```csharp
public static readonly StructureTypeStandard TFoot;
```

aspose-pdf-htmlsaveoptions.rasterimagess.code:
## HtmlSaveOptions.RasterImagesSavingModes enumeration

Converted PDF can contain raster images(.png, *.jpeg etc.) This enum defines methods of how raster images can be handled during conversion of PDF to HTML

```csharp
public enum RasterImagesSavingModes
```

### Values

| Name | Value | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AsPngImagesEmbeddedIntoSvg | `0` | for each distinct raster file will be generated wrapper SVG image, and raster image will be embedded as Base64 encoded strings into that SVG image |
| AsExternalPngFilesReferencedViaSvg | `1` | distinct raster images will be put apart as PNG files but will be referenced through wrapping SVG images, i.e. will be generated one PNG file and one SVG for each raster image, and each of such SVGs will contain links to relevant PNG file |
| AsEmbeddedPartsOfPngPageBackground | `2` | Will be generated one big PNG background file for each result page. Raster images will be embedded into that file and rendered as regions of that image. No external PNG files for each image will be generated, only one PNG file per page will be present in conversion result set of files. |
| DontSave | `3` | Do not save images for Fixed Layout |

aspose-pdf-pdfformatconversionoptions.pu.code:
## PdfFormatConversionOptions.PuaProcessingStrategy enumeration

Some PDF documents have special unicode symbols, which are belonged to Private Use Area (PUA), see description at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Private_Use_Areas. This symbols cause an PDF/A compliant errors like "Text is mapped to Unicode Private Use Area but no ActualText entry is present". This enumeration declares a strategies which can be used to handle PUA symbols.

```csharp
public enum PuaProcessingStrategy
```

### Values

| Name | Value | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| None | `0` | Disable PUA symbol processing. This strategy used by default for PDF/A documents with Level B conformance. |
| SurroundPuaTextWithEmptyActualText | `1` | Inserts marked content block with ActualText entry which contains empty text. This strategy gives good results for documents without marked content blocks. Used by default for PDF/A documents with Level A conformance. |
| SubstitutePuaSymbols | `2` | This strategy works slower than 'SurroundPuaTextWithEmptyActualText' but it can remove PUA compliant errors for documents which can't be handled properly by SurroundPuaTextWithEmptyActualText. PUA symbols are substituted on symbol 'space' or special unicode (some PUA symbols have unicode analogs). Substitution applied not to document's text but to font's internal data ToUnicode so it doesn't affects symbol's vision but it affects symbol's presentation in copy/paste operation system buffer. |

aspose-pdf-epubsaveoptions.recognitionmo.code:
## EpubSaveOptions.RecognitionMode enumeration

When PDF file (that usually has fixed layout) is being converted, the conversion engine tries to perform grouping and multi-level analysis to restore the original document author's intent and produce result in flow layout. This property tunes that conversion for this or that desirable method of recognition of content.

```csharp
public enum RecognitionMode
```

### Values

| Name | Value | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Flow | `0` | Full recognition mode, the engine tries to perform grouping and multi-level analysis to restore the original document author's intent and produce xhtml in flow layout. |
| PdfFlow | `1` | The main idea of this conversion is based on saving "natural" order of content rendering that is formed during processing of pdf documents. In the general cases pdf documents keep top-down, left-right rendering order (see attachment directions.png). This assumption allows to create a single-path algorithm that will transform Aps elements that have positions (fixed-layout) into flow formats like HTML,EPUB,DOC. This mode will be especially useful for converting from PDF(APS) into EPUB, because the EPUB format was developed for e-readers like the Kindle or smart-phones. The size of screen those devices usually is less than the size of screen of ordinary PC. Therefore the content of EPUB documents is better to save in the flow format, for correct rendering on screens with different sizes. In this mode every column will be added to the end of previous column this allows to keep the logical structure of transformed document during "pagination" in EPUB readers. This achievement allows correctly render scientific or magazine articles. |
| Fixed | `2` | This mode is fast and good for maximally preserving the original look pages, but unfortunately many EPUB readeres don't support xhtml with fixed layout |

| Name | Value | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Textbox | `0` | This mode is fast and good for maximally preserving original look of the PDF file, but editability of the resulting document could be limited. |
| Flow | `1` | Full recognition mode, the engine performs grouping and multi-level analysis to restore the original document author's intent and produce a maximally editable document. The downside is that the output document might look different from the original PDF file. |
| EnhancedFlow | `2` | An alternative Flow mode that supports the recognition of tables. |

## Remarks

Use the Textbox mode when the resulting document is not goining to be heavily edited futher. Textboxes are easy to modify when there is not a lot to do.

Use the Flow mode when the output document needs further editing. Paragraphs and texlines in the flow mode allow easy modification of text, but unupported formatting objects will look worse than in the Textbox mode.

aspose-pdf-logicalstructure-structuretyp.code:
## StructureTypeStandard.TOC field

(Table of contents) A list made up of table of contents item entries (structure type TOCI) and/or other nested table of contents entries (TOC).

A TOC entry that includes only TOCI entries represents a flat hierarchy. A TOC entry that includes other nested TOC entries (and possibly TOCI entries) represents a more complex hierarchy.Ideally, the hierarchy of a top level TOC entry reflects the structure of the main body of the document.

```csharp
public static readonly StructureTypeStandard TOC;
```

## StructureTypeStandard.TOCI field

(Table of contents item) An individual member of a table of contents. This entry's children may be any of the following structure types:

**Lbl**

A label

**Reference**

A reference to the title and the page number

**NonStruct**

Non-structure elements for wrapping a leader artifact

**P**

Descriptive text

**TOC**

Table of content elements for hierarchical tables of content, as described for the TOC entry

```csharp
public static readonly StructureTypeStandard TOCI;
```

aspose-pdf-htmlsaveoptions-convertmarked.code:
## HtmlSaveOptions.ConvertMarkedContentToLayers property

If attribute ConvertMarkedContentToLayers set to true then an all elements inside a PDF marked content (layer) will be put into an HTML div with "data-pdflayer" attribute specifying a layer name. This layer name will be extracted from optional properties of PDF marked content. If this attribute is false (by default) then no any layers will be created from PDF marked content.

aspose-pdf-text-textextractionoptions.te.code:
## TextExtractionOptions.TextFormattingMode enumeration

Defines different modes which can be used while converting pdf document into text. See !:TextDevice class.

```csharp
public enum TextFormattingMode
```

### Values

| Name | Value | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Pure | `0` | Represent pdf content with a bit of formatting routines. |
| Raw | `1` | Represent pdf content as is, i.e. without formatting. |
| Flatten | `2` | Represent pdf content with positioning text fragments by their coordinates. It is basically similar to "Raw" mode. But while "Raw" focuses on preserving the structure of text fragments (operators) in a document, "Flatten" focuses on keeping text in the order it is read. |
| MemorySaving | `3` | Extraction with memory saving. It is almost same to 'Raw' mode but works slightly faster and uses less memory. |

aspose-pdf-ai-openaiocrcopilot.code:
## OpenAIOcrCopilot class

Provides OCR capabilities to extract text from PDF documents and images. The supported image types: PNG (.png), JPEG (.jpeg and .jpg), WEBP (.webp), non-animated GIF (.gif). Example usage of creating an OpenAI client, configuring options, and using the OCR copilot.

```csharp
// Create AI client.
var openAiClient = OpenAIClient
    .CreateWithApiKey(ApiKey) // Create OpenAI client with the API key.
    //.WithOrganization("org_123") // Configure optional parameters.
    .Build(); // Build

// Create copilot options.
var options = OpenAIOcrCopilotOptions
    .Create() // Create options like this, or...
    //.Create(options => { options.Model = OpenAIModels.Gpt4O; }) // ...create using delegate.
    .WithDocument("DocumentInputPath"); // .WithDocument methods allows to add Document objects and paths to PDF documents and images.

// Create summary copilot.
IOcrCopilot ocrCopilot = AICopilotFactory.CreateOcrCopilot(openAiClient, options);

// Get text recognitions.
List<TextRecognitionResult> textRecognitions = await ocrCopilot.GetTextRecognitionResultAsync();

// Access to the extracted text.
string text = textRecognitions[0].OcrDetails[0].ExtractedText;
```

```csharp
public class OpenAIOcrCopilot : IOcrCopilot
```

## Compare(Document, Document, string, SideBySideComparisonOptions) {#compare}

Compares two documents. The pages are compared one by one. The pages of the compared documents are copied one after another into the resulting document. Next is the second one from the first document and then the second one from the second document, etc. You can open it in Adobe Acrobat in Two-page view to see the changes side by side. Deletions are noted on the page on the left, and insertions are noted on the page on the right.

aspose-pdf-unifiedsaveoptions-trymergead.code:
## UnifiedSaveOptions.TryMergeAdjacentSameBackgroundImages field

Sometimes PDFs contain background images (of pages or table cells) constructed from several same tiling background images put one near other. In such case renderers of target formats (f.e MsWord for DOCS format) sometimes generates visible boundaries beetween parts of background images, cause their techniques of image edge smoothing (anti-aliasing) is different from Acrobat Reader. If it looks like exported document contains such visible boundaries between parts of same background images, please try use this setting to get rid of that unwanted effect. ATTENTION! This optimization of quality usually essentially slows down conversion, so, please, use this option only when it's really necessary.

```csharp
public bool TryMergeAdjacentSameBackgroundImages;
```

aspose-pdf-htmlsaveoptions-explicitlisto.code:
## HtmlSaveOptions.ExplicitListOfSavedPages property

With this property You can explicitely define what pages of document should be converted. Pages in this list must have 1-based numbers. I.e. valid numbers of pages must be taken from range (1...[NumberOfPagesInConvertedDocument]) Order of appearing of pages in this list does not affect their order in result HTML page(s) - in result pages allways will go in order in which they are present in source PDF. If this list is null (as it is by default), all pages will be converted. If any page number of this list will go out of range of present pages(1-[amountOfPagesInDocument]) exception will be thrown.

```csharp
public int[] ExplicitListOfSavedPages { get; set; }
```

aspose-pdf-htmlsaveoptions-splitcssintop.code:
## HtmlSaveOptions.SplitCssIntoPages property

When multipage-mode selected(i.e 'SplitIntoPages' is 'true'), then this attribute defines whether should be created separate CSS-file for each result HTML page. By default this attribute is false, so, will be created one big common CSS for all created pages. Summary size of all CSSes generated in this mode(one CSS per page) usually much more than size of one big CSS file, because in former case CSS classes are duplicates in such case in several CSS files for each page. So, this setting is worse to be used only when You are interested in future processing of each HTML page independently, and therefore size of CSS of each one page taken apart is the most critical issue.

```csharp
public bool SplitCssIntoPages { get; set; }
```

aspose-pdf-htmlsaveoptions-pagesflowtype.code:
## HtmlSaveOptions.PagesFlowTypeDependsOnViewersScreenSize field

If attribute 'SplitOnPages=false', than whole HTML representing all input PDF pages will be put into one big result HTML file. This flag defines whether result HTML will be generated in such way that flow of areas that represent PDF pages in result HTML will depend on screen resolution of viewer. Suppose width of screen on viewer side is big enough to put 2 or more pages one near other in horizontal direction. If this flag set to true, then this opportunity will be used (as many pages will be shown in horizontal direction one near another as it possible, then next horizontal group of pages will be shown under first one ). Otherwise pages will flow in such way: next page goes always under previous one.

```csharp
public bool PagesFlowTypeDependsOnViewersScreenSize;
```

aspose-pdf-pdfformatconversionoptions.re.code:
## PdfFormatConversionOptions.RemoveFontsStrategy enumeration

Some documens have large size after converison into PDF/A format. To reduce file size for these documents it's necessary to define a strategy of fonts removing. This enumeration declares a strategies which can be used to optimize fonts usage. Every strategy from this enumeration has sense only when flag `OptimizeFileSize` is set.

```csharp
[Flags]
public enum RemoveFontsStrategy : byte
```

### Values

| Name | Value | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| RemoveDuplicatedFonts | `4` | This strategy removes all the fonts which have duplicates in document. If document contains group of duplicated fonts only one font from this group is embedded in document. All other fonts from this group are removed from document, every removed font substituted with the already embedded analog. |
| RemoveSimilarFontsWithDifferentWidths | `1` | This strategy looks like RemoveDuplicatedFonts but it removes not fully duplicated fonts but fonts which are similar one to another and differs only by parameter "Widths". This parameter contains set of some widths for specified symbols of font. Every value of width from this "Widths" set is not the real width of symbol(glyph), real width for this symbol already defined in font's binary data. Value of width from "Widths" set means visual width for this symbol - the width which PDF viewer software must set on displaying symbol instead of real width defined in the font. More accurately specification tells: Acrobat 5.0 and later viewers use the glyph widths stored in the font dictionary to override the widths of glyphs in the font program itself, which improves the consistency of the display and printing of the document. This strategy is more effective than RemoveDuplicatedFonts but using of this strategy in some cases theoretically could damage visual presentation of converted document. This defect is possible due to that declared widths of fonts could be different for the same symbol
